name: Soft-Shell Crabs with Lemon & Capers
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a small sharp knife, peel one of the lemons, removing all of the bitter white pith. Working over a bowl, cut in between the membranes to release the sections. Squeeze the juice from the remaining lemon into the bowl of sections and set aside.
2. Preheat the oven to 250°F.
3. On a large plate, combine the flour with the pepper.
4. In a large nonreactive, nonstick skillet, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over moderately high heat. Pat the crabs dry with paper towels and dredge them in the flour mixture.
5. Add 4 of the crabs to the skillet and cook, turning once, until red and crisp, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer them to a heatproof platter and place in the oven to keep warm.
6. Repeat with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil and 4 crabs.
7. Add the butter and shallots to the skillet and cook over moderately high heat, stirring frequently, until the shallots have softened, 3 to 4 minutes.
8. Increase the heat to high, add the wine, and simmer until reduced by half, about 2 minutes.
9. Add the reserved lemon sections and juice, and the capers; stir well.
10. Place 2 crabs on each plate and spoon the sauce over them.
